title: Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homelessness Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homelessness Issues 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Navigation center offers clothing, toiletries, restrooms, light snacks, and beverages, bus passes (10 per month, by availability), internet access, device charging station, a community phone, and a temporary mailing address. Also provides a secure location for medication storage, including refrigeration and Narcan/Naloxone on site. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Medication storage: Unsheltered individuals All other services: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH ASSISTANCE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach to individuals experiencing homelessness. Distributes camping supplies, food, clothing, and medical and hygiene supplies. Also offers case management for older adults and individuals with disabilities who are unable to navigate resources. Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness, including people living in places not meant for habilitation Hours: Hours vary by location Email (802) 704-7280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASS OUTREACH NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Partners with local churches to provide hot lunches and a bi-monthly medical clinic. Other services include case management, hygiene supplies, temporary mailing address, and phone messaging. Replacement ID services are available in exchange for volunteer service. Tents, tarps, and blankets offered when available and in exchange for volunteer service (can be waived if needed). Bus passes are available for court or parole meetings in Coquille. Local bus passes are available on a case-by-case basis. 1200 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: -Unrestricted; priority is given to people experiencing homelessness with mental health conditions -Bus passes: Local transportation to Coquille for court or parole meetings. Must show summons or ticket. -Local bus passes on a case-by-case basis -Volunteer time may be requested for high-demand or limited-availability items, on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 11am-12:30pm at Gloria Dei Lutheran Church, 1290 Thompson Road Coos Bay, OR 97240 Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 11am-12:30pm at Harmony Methodist Church, 123 Ocean Boulevard SE, Coos Bay, OR 97420 Email (541) 888-3202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF THE CITY HOMELESS SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Distributes personal care items such as toothbrushes, deodorant, and snacks. Coats, sleeping bags & tents. Also provides specialized information and referral, including referrals to counseling resources. 28925 SW Boberg Road Wilsonville, OR 97070 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (503) 582-8680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a community hygiene, sanitation, and safety hub. Provides showers, bedding in exchange for soiled, a clothing swap, menstruation and moontime hygiene supplies, first-aid kits, bathrooms, legal disposal for household trash and needles, foot, wound, and mouth care, and a warm outdoor space with coffee and tea. Also offers paid work and opportunities for advocacy. 824 SE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 3pm-8:30pm Open regardless of weather. Last shower 45 minutes before close. Search for Hygiene4all Hub on Google Maps for unanticipated holiday or other closures. Email (971) 236-2524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER PERSONAL GROOMING SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides hygiene and feminine products, children’s diapers, and incontinence supplies. Accepts donations 2555 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Hygiene kits: People experiencing homelessness Diapers: Children Incontinence Supplies: Adults Hours: Monday 10am-2pm and 4pm-7pm, Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 673-5918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in accessing services and agencies to help people transition out of homelessness into stable housing. The center offers showers, laundry, haircuts, clothing, lockers, and mailing address service. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm. All service intakes end 15 minutes before close. 1st Thursday and 3rd Tuesday of the month the Resource Center closes at noon. Restroom and showers: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm Clothing: Monday/Friday 10am-11am, Wednesday 1pm-2pm Haircuts: Wednesday-Friday, times vary Mailroom: Monday-Friday 8:30am-10am and 12:45pm-3:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE COMPASS OUTREACH NAVIGATION CENTER HEART OF THE CITY HOMELESS SUPPORT SERVICES HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER PERSONAL GROOMING SUPPLIES ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER
